title: Roommate Conflict Resolution
transcript: Student A: Hey, can we talk about something? It's about the apartment. Student B: Sure, what's going on? Student A: Well, I've noticed that the dishes have been piling up in the sink for days. I usually end up washing them, and it's getting a bit frustrating. Student B: Oh, I'm sorry. I've been really busy with midterms and honestly just forgot. I'll make sure to clean up after myself. Student A: Thanks, I appreciate that. Also, could you let me know if you're having friends over? I have an early class on Tuesdays and the noise keeps me up. Student B: Of course, I'll text you in advance. By the way, would it be okay if I used your blender sometime? Mine broke last week. Student A: Yeah, just ask me before you use it so I know where it is. Student B: Deal. Thanks for bringing this up. I want us to get along.

questions.stem: Why does Student A want to talk to Student B?
options: To complain about the noise from a party
To discuss apartment issues and set boundaries
To ask for help with midterm preparation
questions.explanation: Student A brings up the dirty dishes and late-night noise to resolve apartment issues and set boundaries.

questions.stem: What does Student B request from Student A?
options: A ride to campus in the morning
questions.explanation: Student B asks if they can use Student A's blender since theirs broke.

Deal. Thanks for bringing this up. I want us to get along.
